verses.text: 'O come, let us sing unto the LORD,' making a joyful noise to the Rock of our salvation, coming before Him with thanksgiving. Worship is a glad gathering. The redeemed are summoned to praise the God who saves them.

'O come, let us sing unto the LORD,' making a joyful noise to the Rock of our salvation, coming before Him with thanksgiving. Worship is a glad gathering. The redeemed are summoned to praise the God who saves them.
For 'the LORD is a great God,' holding the depths and heights of the earth, the sea and dry land He made. All creation is His. The greatness of the Maker calls forth the worship of His creatures.
For 'the LORD is a great God,' holding the depths and heights of the earth, the sea and dry land He made. All creation is His. The greatness of the Maker calls forth the worship of His creatures.
For 'the LORD is a great God,' holding the depths and heights of the earth, the sea and dry land He made. All creation is His. The greatness of the Maker calls forth the worship of His creatures.
'O come, let us worship and bow down,' for He is our God and 'we are the people of his pasture.' Reverent humility befits us. The flock bows gladly before the Shepherd who keeps them.
'O come, let us worship and bow down,' for He is our God and 'we are the people of his pasture.' Reverent humility befits us. The flock bows gladly before the Shepherd who keeps them.

'To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your heart' as Israel did, who through unbelief could not enter God's rest. Warning follows worship. The rest God offers is lost by the hardness that refuses to believe.
